author: @carlinrackley8547
text:
If this rumor turns out to be true, it'll basically lock me out completely. My flip phone has a basic camera, but no front-facing/selfie lens, I don't own any credit/debit cards (only gift cards), and I absolutely won't upload my ID to any website—I don't trust them not to store it permanently, sell it, or get hacked, leading to targeted scams or identity theft.

  Congress needs to stop pushing laws that mandate ID for basic internet use. The classic excuse is 'But the children!'—but it's not the government's job to police kids online. That's on the parents, guardians, and teachers. These 'protection' laws always end up hurting adults and limiting everyone's freedom in the long run.

Privacy matters more than performative safety theater.
